channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Cassette Beasts 2002 jumps to a new millennium of beast fusing and battling
item.content:encoded:
Bytten Studio and publisher Raw Fury have announced Cassette Beasts 2002, a sequel to the acclaimed creature collector and battler from 2023. It’s coming to PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2 and PC. All the good stuff.

Cassette Beasts 2002 isn’t the 2002nd entry in the series, it’s more indicative of the setting. Now taking place in London in 2002, there’s a new era of monster-collecting to get up to, recording, battling and fusing over 250 beasts, playing solo or in multiplayer. You’ll be working to unmask a mysterious occultists that threatens the balance of the world around you.

For our Cassette Beasts review, Nic said, “Cassette Beasts is simply one of the best Pokémon-like games I’ve come across, and I’m looking forward to earning a 100% completion. With a massive end-game and modder support built in at release though, I feel like I’m going to get very sidetracked on the way there.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/casette-beasts-2002-jumps-to-a-new-millennium-of-beast-fusing-and-battling/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Outward 2 Drops its Open Beta Today Ahead of Early Access Launch in July 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last month, we learned that Outward 2 would be launching in early access on July 7, 2026, and today, as part of the PC Gaming Show during Summer Game Fest 2026, we got another look at the game and the reveal that an open beta is now live for players to jump into ahead of launch.

Several players already looking forward to Outward 2 are in the currently running closed beta, but now anyone who didn't make it into the closed beta, or anyone who simply likes the look of Outward 2 can give it a shot on Steam to help pressure-test the game's servers beginning from now until the game arrives next month.

You'll also want to test the game's servers with a friend beside you, as the open beta trailer also showed off co-op play, including split-screen co-op for anyone who wants to pull up an extra chair to their PC for their friend to join.

The studio is also kicking off a competition for players to win two early access keys for Outward 2, ahead of its release next month. Beginning today and running until June 25, 2026, players can enter the competition by first following one of the game's social media accounts, be it Twitter, TikTok, Instagram, subscribing to its YouTube channel or joining the game's Discord.

Share a screenshot or a video from your time in the open beta that shows off either "things going really well or going hilariously wrong," the studio outlined in a press release. Add #ExploreAurai and mention someone you'd play the game with and tag Nine Dots Studio to complete your entry. There's also a contest page you can visit if you have any major questions.

When Wccftech spoke to Nine Dots Studio about Outward 2 back in 2024, chief executive officer Guillaume Boucher-Vidal promised that Outward 2 would be "as cruel and obscure as the first one. I insist on that. I'm quite okay that it's gonna turn off some people because there are others who are turned on by exactly the thing that is turning off others."

Today, everyone who has been waiting for this game for the last seven years since the first game's launch will finally be able to put that to the test, and then again in the early access release. We'll see if Boucher-Vidal and the rest of the Nine Dots Studio team has lived up to that expectation.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/outward-2-open-beta-reveal-release-date-summer-game-fest-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #826 Hints and Answers for Today, June 7, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #826 for Sunday, June 7, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 7, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #826 are all terms related to reptiles.

General Strands Hints for June 7

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Jake Roberts.
  • Hint 2: Lockjaw from Hey Arnold.
  • Hint 3: Term used to describe killers.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #825 for June 6, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 5, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • NAKED
  • FAKE
  • FAKED
  • LAME
  • BONE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #826 On June 7, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • BULLFROG
  • CROCODILE
  • CHAMELEON
  • TURTLE
  • SNAKE
  • SPANAGRAM: COLDBLOODED

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #825?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 7 will be Strands Game #826.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-826-hints-and-answers-for-today-june-7-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Where Winds Meet now available for Xbox Series
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Free-to-play open-world action adventure RPG gets a surprise Xbox release.

Everstone Studio has released free-to-play open-world action adventure RPG Where Winds Meet for Xbox Series and PC via Microsoft Store. The developer also shared a sneak peek at the upcoming third major expansion, “Hidden Mountain.”

Where Winds Meet previously launched for PlayStation 5, PC via Steam and Epic Games Store, iOS via App Store, and Android via Google Play.

Here are the latest details, via Everstone Studio:

About

Where Winds Meet is an epic open-world Wuxia action RPG that lets players forge their own legend in 10th-century China, battling with distinctive martial arts and unique weapons across a breathtaking, seamless landscape in rich solo, cooperative, and player-versus-player adventures.

The Horizon Expands: First Look at the “Hidden Mountain” Expansion

Fresh off the success of the “Hexi” and “Imperial Palace” expansions, the newly revealed “Hidden Mountain” expansion continues the mission to offer players a different gaming experience.

  • The 3D Vertical Landscape – This massive new region introduces a breathtaking, multi-layered landscape that further expands the open-world exploration, rewarding curious players with hidden surprises, deep secrets, and traversal challenges at every elevation.
  • The Mysterious Mohist Sect – Perched high among these treacherous peaks lies the secretive Mohist Sect. The introduction of this legendary faction brings entirely new mysteries, advanced martial arts mechanics, unique gameplay loops, and major narrative twists to the ongoing Wuxia epic.
  • New Hand Guard Discipline – This fast-paced martial art that players have long anticipated for delivers explosive close-quarters combat. Blending rapid pursuits with relentless counter-attacks when enemies slip up, it channels accumulated inner will into a single, devastating blow capable of shattering anything in its path.

Built Exclusively for the Xbox Living Room Experience

To celebrate its console debut, Where Winds Meet boasts extensive hardware optimization for peak performance, alongside a custom controller collaboration and exclusive rewards for Xbox Game Pass members. Key highlights include:

  • Custom-Designed Controller – An exclusive, custom-designed Xbox Wireless Controller, meticulously styled to capture one of the game’s most emotionally resonant and iconic images, and reflect the elegant yet lethal aesthetics of the Wuxia universe.
  • Xbox Optimization – Full Smart Delivery support with an overhauled UI and enlarged text scaling for couch-friendly readability. Features full HDR, AMD FSR 2 (4K upscale on Series X / 2K upscale on Series S), and deep memory optimizations for stable, fluid frame rates during high-speed exploration and intense sword clashes.
  • Day One Game Pass Ultimate Perks – Xbox Game Pass members can claim a rich starter pack. Phase 1 starting from June 7 to July 23 (PT) featuring 450 Echo Jade, 90,000 Coins, and essential progression items like Attune Stones, Lingering Melodies and Pillow Pills.

Watch a new trailer below.

Xbox Series Launch Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/where-winds-meet-now-available-for-xbox-series
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: DOOM: The Dark Ages DLC ‘Revelations’ announced
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Due out on July 7 alongside "Riptaorium 3.0" update.

Publisher Bethesda Softworks and developer id Software have announced DOOM: The Dark Ages paid downloadable content “Revelations” and the free “Ripatorium 3.0” update. Both will launch on July 7.

Here are the details:

“Revelations” (priced at $19.99 standalone, and included with the Digital Upgrade, Premium Edition, or Collector’s Bundle) introduces a whole new dimension of combat: The all-new deadly Chain Spear offers returning Slayers a uniquely satisfying combat system—one that rewards mastery with enhanced power and mobility. Featuring new levels with deeper puzzles, new demons, and new mysteries, the downloadable content delivers a breadth of new content for players to experience.

Along with “Revelations” comes the Ripatorium 3.0 update, free for all existing players. This update adds deeper customization, improved pass code generation, and saving and loading personal presets. Premium Edition or Collectors Bundle owners can access 3 new maps, new demons, and fully upgraded new weapons after completing the downloadable content.

DOOM: The Dark Ages is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store. It is also available via Game Pass.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

DLC “Revelations” Announce Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/doom-the-dark-ages-dlc-revelations-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sega Confirms And Responds To Generative AI Content In New Crazy Taxi Game
item.content:encoded:
Crazy Taxi: World Tour was just announced at the Xbox Games Showcase and players were quick to notice that the game's Steam page contained a generative AI disclaimer which reads, "At SEGA Corporation, we utilize generative AI as a support tool for developers, aiming to provide better content to our users and enable developers to focus more on creative tasks We have used such generative AI support tools during development of Crazy Taxi: World Tour. No AI was used in reference to the performers in the game."

We reached out to Sega for confirmation and a potential response to the details on the Steam page. Many fans excited for the return of Sega's racing game have already expressed frustration over the use of generative AI assets. Below is Sega's response, which repeats some of the details on the Steam page:

At SEGA Corporation, generative AI is available as an optional support tool for developers, enabling our teams to focus more on creative tasks and ultimately focus on what matters most: delivering better games to our consumers.

Generative AI was used to support our teams during the development of background assets for ”Crazy Taxi: World Tour”. Assets generated were still subject to review by the development team.

No AI was used in reference to the performers in the game.

To learn more about Crazy Taxi: World Tour's reveal during the Xbox Games Showcase, head here.



https://gameinformer.com/2026/06/07/sega-confirms-and-responds-to-generative-ai-content-in-new-crazy-taxi-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Signet City Is A New 'Fungalpunk' RPG From The Creator Of Citizen Sleeper Where You Play As A Parasite
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Fellow Traveler and Citizen Sleeper creator Gareth Damian Martin have unveiled Signit City, a first-person "fungalpunk" RPG where you play as a parasite. There's no release date for it yet, but Fellow Traveler says it's "coming soon" to PC. 

In Signet City, you control a parasite in the titular coastal city, which is in sharp decline because in this world, the biological computer has outgrown the silicon chip. Born in the brackish waters of Signet City's bay, you must inhabit human minds and guide your hosts into their final season. "From the stained wallpaper of the squats to the towering monuments that dot its skyline, grow through and into this strange city, changing it forever," a press release reads. It looks awesome, and considering how great both Citizen Sleeper and Citizen Sleeper 2: Starward Vector are, we're keeping our eyes on Signet City.

Check it out for yourself in the Signet City reveal trailer below: 

Fellow Traveler says Signet City blends first-person exploration with tabletop-inspired narrative RPG design in a world inspired by post-punk 1980s Britain and weird fiction. Because you play as a parasite – decidedly not human – you can move through the city's social body, shape conversations how you see fit, influence Signet City inhabitants, and more, according to a press release. Notably, all of this will happen in stark black-and-white, a nod to the game's visual identity, which is rooted in brutalist architecture and post-punk movements. 

Here are some additional screenshots: 

Signet City is coming to PC soon. 

What do you think of this new direction for the creator of Citizen Sleeper? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/07/signet-city-is-a-new-fungalpunk-rpg-from-the-creator-of-citizen-sleeper-where
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: There Are No Ghosts at the Grand gets a very musical Steam demo
item.content:encoded:
A new trailer for There Are No Ghosts at the Grand has dropped during the PC Gaming Show, introducing a new character and announcing a demo is now live on Steam.

Meet PC Adam Watson, the local copper who’s rather keen to show you around and a bit of a braggard… and someone that will die in 30 days.

Meeting him is just the first part of a trailer that then delves into musical, horror action, redecorating, and hanging out with a talking cat.

I got to check out the game last year following its summer announcement, and thoroughly enjoyed its off-kilter mix of musical, hotel renovation and mild terror. I said, “After the music-filled announcement and the otherworldly twists that tail-ended that trailer, seeing the game in action, with the oh-so-easy to use redecorating tools, the characters and the ways that they’re brought together in inventive ways is a true delight. There Are No Ghosts at the Grand deserves its place on your wishlist for 2026.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/there-are-no-ghosts-at-the-grand-gets-a-very-musical-steam-demo/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Company of Heroes Definitive Edition remasters the classic RTS
item.content:encoded:
Relic has announce Company of Heroes Definitive Edition, a modernising remaster of the classic WW2 real time strategy game coming to PC later this year..

The definitive edition includes the base game, and the Opposing Fronts and Tales of Valor expansion, to make for a huge RTS bundle, but the most important aspects of it are technical improvements and visual sprucing up.

Similar to the Dawn of War remaster last year, Company of Heroes has been upgraded to 64-bit to be more compatible with modern platforms. From that baseline, Relic is then enhancing the visuals with improved lighting, shadows, water and reflections. The goal is to enhance but retain the original art style.

The following quality of life improvements and assurances are also coming:

  • Enhanced A.I. delivers a more dynamic and challenging experience, with a new Ruthless difficulty option for the most hardened strategists
  • Modernized User Interface and controls, including grid key support and fully customizable hotkeys
  • Gameplay improvements such as vehicle reversing and automatic unit reinforcement at HQ
  • Widescreen and ultrawide support with an optimized HUD and screen layout
  • Full mod compatibility, supporting a 20-year catalogue of community-created content

“Company of Heroes – Definitive Edition celebrates the game that started it all and preserves its legacy for the next generation of players,” says Justin Dowdeswell, CEO at Relic Entertainment. “Company of Heroes is one of the most beloved strategy games ever made, and we want new players to experience it at its best, while giving our long-time fans the definitive way to relive it.”

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/company-of-heroes-definitive-edition-remasters-the-classic-rts/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: inXile Seals Clockwork Revolution’s Xbox Console Exclusivity, Locks 2027 Launch Window; Time-Travel Rewrites Will Reshape Everything (Including Allies
item.content:encoded:
Clockwork Revolution, the first-person action RPG developed by inXile Entertainment, received a launch window during the Xbox Games Showcase 2026: the game will be available next year, and it will be an Xbox console exclusive, just like Gears of War: E-Day. Of course, it's also going to be available on PC via Steam and the Xbox store, and it will be on Game Pass and support Xbox Play Anywhere.

inXile shared some new info, too. The game is set in the steampunk city of Avalon, a city that looks polished and prosperous on the surface but is driven by industry and ambition, with its costs paid by the people at the bottom. It's a layered city with contrasting districts, like the ash-choked streets in the Tangle, where crime bosses trade in foul-mouthed automatons, and places like the Burning House exist as establishments that soothe clientele with performers like a character named Commodity. The lavish promenades where the city pretends to be something better are contrasted directly with the working-class grime underneath.

In Clockwork Revolution, you play as Morgan Vanette, a rogue thief whose look, skills, and choices can be shaped throughout the game. The version of Morgan shown in the trailer is just one version, not the canonical one, as players will customize who Morgan is at the start and who Morgan becomes through their decisions. This makes the character a fully player-defined protagonist rather than a preset hero.

A key companion introduced today is Prentice, a flying automaton who joins Morgan very early in the game. She is described as "part observer, part companion" who guides Morgan through the branching realities you create with your time travel. Importantly, she is not just a narrative device:  she has her own abilities and skill tree, and she opens new ways to interact with the world. Her connection to Morgan is tied to how you end up with the Chronometer, the device that makes time travel possible.

This is the central gameplay tool, allowing Morgan to travel between past and present to rewrite key moments. As the game progresses, it can unlock new abilities, such as Displace, which lets you instantly reposition certain objects in the environment. This can open paths, solve problems, or turn the environment into a weapon — the trailer shows it used to launch an explosive barrel into a group of enemies. This is, of course, just one example, and more abilities will be revealed before launch.

The Allies and the Villain

Morgan is part of a gang called the Rotten Row Hooligans, of which inXile introduced five members by name: Ulysses, Nazim, Erasmus, Hazel, and Anne. In the Tangle, Avalon's rougher district, having a gang at your back is necessary, not optional. Crucially, these characters are not companion characters that follow you around; they have their own lives, their own stories, and plenty to lose when Morgan starts messing with time. When you reach into the past and pull threads, their lives change in ways you don't always see coming, and the crew you come back to may not be the same one you left.

The game's key antagonist is Lady Ironwood, who rules Avalon with ruthless precision. She is already using time travel herself to maintain her grip on power, reshaping the past to ensure her future stays exactly the way she wants it. Once Morgan begins uncovering what she has done, her Industrial Secret Service agents will begin pursuing you. A strong theme is that choices have butterfly effects. Some consequences are immediate, some are big, and some take a long time to catch up with you, but the developers promise that every choice will matter.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/clockwork-revolution-2027-xbox-console-exclusive/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gorgeous Slice-Of-Life Game Vivarium Brings That Old-School Anime Energy
item.content:encoded:
For me, one of the best surprises at today’s Xbox showcase was the game Vivarium, a new slice-of-life game that looks like a gorgeous early ’90s anime.

This is so dang cute! From what I can piece together here, this is a game about a world condensed inside an abandoned terrarium, and in that world lives a child named Jenny in a rural town filled with nature. Jenny does a lot of exploring in and around the town, and finds a tree sprout that she’s hoping will grow quickly. There are adorable anthropomorphic animal townspeople, seemingly some sort of farming and crafting system, and also maybe some sort of mystery to solve?

But what I love about it is the style: it really does look like an anime, not just in the animation style but also in the way it’s presented, with dialogue displayed as if it’s subtitles and very little in the way of UI to be seen in the trailer. beyond a clock in the top-right that’s giving Animal Crossing. It’s hard to tell here, but it even seems like there’s a lot of attention paid to the way little animated snippets appear in between gameplay that make it look like you’re watching a slice-of-life anime about a kid on summer vacation.

We’ve got some time to wait before we can really sink into Vivarium, as it’s not coming until 2027. It’ll be on Xbox and PC when it does.



https://kotaku.com/vivarium-game-xbox-life-sim-old-school-anime-energy-2000703752
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: WWE Applied for Two New Gaming Trademarks in May 2026
item.content:encoded:
WWE filed two new video game-related trademarks back in May 2026.

On May 10, WWE filed to trademark the term “WWE Superstar Scramble.” Then, on May 18, the company filed to trademark “WWE Hard Hitters.” Both trademarks have the same description, noting that they’re used for video games.

“Trademark registration is intended to cover the categories of video and computer game tapes, video and computer game discs, video and computer game cassettes, video and computer game cartridges, video and computer game CD-roms, video output game machines for use with televisions; video and computer game software; video games for use with wireless devices; online video games; video games for use via a global computer network and wireless devices; coin-fed amusement gaming machines; interactive video game programs and computer game cartridges; interactive multimedia computer games on all platforms comprised of computer hardware and computer game software; electronic games that involves interaction with a user interface to generate visual feedback on a video device; video and computer game tapes, video and computer game discs, video and computer game cassettes, video and computer game cartridgesVideo and computer game tapes, video and computer game discs, video and computer game cassettes, video and computer game cartridges, video and computer game CD-roms, video output game machines for use with televisions; video and computer game tapes, video and computer game discs, video and computer game cassettes, video and computer game cartridges,” the official trademark description reads.

WWE Has Released One Game Per Year for Decades

Earlier this year, WWE filed for three separate gaming-related trademarks. These trademarks suggested a potential generational clash between WWE Superstars. However, WWE has not announced any game projects beyond its annual console release. You can learn more about these trademarks here.

2026 marks the 20th anniversary of WWE SmackDown vs. Raw 2007. This was the first WWE game released on the Xbox 360. With that, it’s been 20 years since WWE stopped releasing different experiences for each gaming console and instead began releasing a single, uniform experience annually.

It is not certain whether these trademarks will lead to a new video game release, and if that game will be released on mobile or console devices. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Learn more about the latest WWE release, WWE 2K26, and the surprising inclusion of Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick as part of the roster at this link.

What would you like to see from WWE games in the future? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/wwe-applied-for-two-new-gaming-trademarks-in-may-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Becomes First $1 Billion Film of 2026
item.content:encoded:
The Super Mario Galaxy Movie became the first movie to gross $1 billion at the box office in 2026.

As first reported by The Hollywood Reporter, the movie has generated $428.5 million in the United States and $571.5 million internationally. Its predecessor, the 2023 Super Mario Bros. Movie, earned over $1.3 billion at the box office. This means that, between the two films, Mario has become one of Hollywood’s most lucrative animated film series.

According to the report, the Mario franchise ranks ninth among animated juggernauts, with $2.3 billion generated across both films and Global ticket sales. It joins the likes of Kung Fu Panda, Inside Out, and Despicable Me. Of course, the Despicable Me franchise was also created by Illumination and gave birth to the Minions.

What’s Next for the Mario Franchise in Film and Games?

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie was the main event of Mario’s 40th anniversary. Unfortunately, most Mario game lovers are waiting for the announcement of the next 3D Mario experience, considering it’s been nearly 10 years since the release of Super Mario Odyssey on the Nintendo Switch. The Galaxy Movie took a considerable amount of direction from Odyssey, as it served as a mishmash of many of Mario’s adventures from the last 40 years.

Characters like Wart and Birdo from Super Mario Bros 2 made their big-screen debut, and there were even some other Nintendo standouts that were featured in cameos, such as Fox McCloud from the Star Fox series.

Shigeru Miyamoto, the father of the Mario series, has confirmed that some of the changes made in the film will carry over into gaming lore. Fans can read more about that here.

As for when we might see the next Mario game, a Nintendo Direct is rumored to be scheduled for the coming week. Prominent leaker NateTheHate has dated the next Mario game for a likely 2027 release. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about the success of the Mario movie franchise? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/super-mario-galaxy-movie-becomes-first-1-billion-film-of-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wo Long 2: Wings of Ember announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Team NINJA have announced action RPG sequel Wo Long 2: Wings of Ember for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC (Steam, Microsoft Store). It will launch in early 2027. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Here is an overview of the game, via Koei Tecmo:

Set in the late Eastern Han period, when various warlords contended for supremacy and the heroes of the Three Kingdoms emerged, a phoenix rises from the fires of war as a nameless champion fights across vast battlefields faced with powerful enemies and fearsome fiends.

In this long-awaited sequel to Wo Long: Fallen Dynasty, which has surpassed over 5 million players worldwide, players must master the evolved Chinese martial arts and conquer battlefields of the Three Kingdoms overrun by monstrous demons alongside renowned heroes who left their mark on history.

To celebrate the announcement, the first game, Wo Long: Fallen Dynasty, is once again available via Game Pass for Xbox Series, Xbox One, and PC.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/wo-long-2-wings-of-ember-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Spyro The Dragon Reignites With A Brand New Entry On Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Take flight in 2027

Publisher Activision and developer Toys for Bob have announced Spyro: A Realm Beyond for the Switch 2; the first brand-new mainline Spyro game in, well... bloomin' yonks. Since 2008, if you don't count the remake trilogy.

Described as Spyro's "boldest evolution yet", the new game appears to be more open than previous entries, with the trailer showcasing a brief snippet of gameplay in which the protagonist is flying freely through the air, shooting fireballs at targets as he goes.

Here's a look at the official description from the trailer:

"When Spyro finds himself stranded, his journey to discover a way home is interrupted by the arrival of a vicious invading force known as the Scavs. Spyro must make new allies and rise to protect this realm before it’s changed forever.

"Feel the freedom of flight as you soar above vibrant landscapes, weave between treetops, dive off massive landmarks and wing your way through this new realm in Spyro’s boldest evolution yet.

"Carve your path through the skies and chain together dives, climbs and tight turns as you navigate the world your way. Crafted by Toys For Bob, this is a new evolution of Spyro, built to send the purple dragon soaring higher than ever before."

There's no date yet beyond the current 2027 release window, but we're honestly thrilled to see the little guy back in a proper mainline entry. Toys for Bob did a fabulous job with the Reignited Trilogy, so we have high hopes for this one.

We'll provide more updates on A Realm Beyond as soon as we hear more.

Will you be checking out the new Spyro adventure in 2027? Share your thoughts with a comment down below.

[source youtube.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/spyro-the-dragon-reignites-with-a-brand-new-entry-on-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crazy Taxi: World Tour Officially Announced For Switch 2, Launching 2027
item.content:encoded:
Fare play

Sega has finally – finally – shown off its revival for the Crazy Taxi series: Crazy Taxi: World Tour.

Featuring music from The Offspring (because of course), the game utilises a very similar art style to the original, which is just wonderful. What's not so great, however, is that it seems to have been developed with the help of generative AI (thanks for the spot, Push Square).

The disclaimer over on Steam reads:

At SEGA Corporation, we utilize generative AI as a support tool for developers, aiming to provide better content to our users and enable developers to focus more on creative tasks.

We have used such generative AI support tools during development of Crazy Taxi: World Tour. No AI was used in reference to the performers in the game.

Okay, so it doesn't necessarily sound like generative AI will actually be implemented into the final product, but it's still nevertheless something to keep in mind if you're bothered about that kind of stuff.

Otherwise, get ready to jump back into the drivers seat when World Tour launches in 2027.

What do you make of this? Will you be picking it up? Let us know with a comment.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/crazy-taxi-world-tour-officially-announced-for-switch-2-launching-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day Direct – everything we now know
item.content:encoded:
Following on from the gameplay reveal that started the Xbox Showcase, and the announcement that Gears of War: E-Day will now be an Xbox console exclusive, and not coming to PS5, the Gears of War: E-Day Direct was a deeper dive into how The Coalition has built this game and everything within.

There’s a lot of behind the scenes details – like how they found the same model of chainsaw as from the original game to recapture the sound – but we’re going to stick to the main gameplay features and information. You can always watch the full E-Day Direct to catch all the info.

Emergence Day in One City

Gears of War E-Day isn’t a globe-trotting adventure across Sera, but will instead see the events of Emergence Day how they unfold in the single city of Kalona across three days.

The game starts with Marcus and Dom reminiscing about Dom’s brother, who died in the Pendulum Wars that had just come to an end, but as news of tremors take over the new channels and then the Locust start to crawl out of the ground, they leap into action. At various points in the game, you’ll be able to take on side mission content from situations that you stumble across, such as COGs needing a bit of help or civilians in need.

While the game is not an open world, The Coalition has approached building it as though it is one. You’ll have a linear level to follow, but it takes place in a fully realised city which morphs and changes with massive destruction events, and the camera can pull back to show the whole vista.

Bravo Squad and a Chainsaw

Bravo Squad is more than just Marcus and Dom, and they’re joined by Mags Carter (played by Elizabeth Ludlow) and Lucas Reyes (played by Jake Ryan Lozano). Mags is a former Gear now working at Kalona’s Imulsion Refinery, while Lucas is a young cadet and comms officer who never saw the front lines.

There’s also some familiar faces returning, like Tai Kaliso from Gears 2. His appearance is the catalyst for the Lancer’s iconic chainsaw attachment – yes, that’s right, Gears of War E-Day will tell the origin story of a chainsaw gun.

The full game can be played in four-player co-op using these four characters, with support for two-player local split-screen retained.

Marcus can jump now

Taking the jump to Unreal Engine 5 as an opportunity for a blank slate for the gameplay. It will naturally retain the same cover shooter action, but cover transitions will be smoother, sprinting can now lead into sliding under objects and around corners, and climbing is now more broadly built into the environments to enable greater vertical spaces.

But also… you can jump now, hopping across gaps within the environment.

Weapons have also been rebuilt around their core principles, so the Gnasher is ultra-close range, the Longshot needs precision at distance. There’s new additions, though, such as the Gut Puncher grenade launcher that can fire with a player-controlled charge.

Multiplayer

The classic 4v4 game modes are back, with levels built throughout Kalona, but there’s also a new Horde Siege mode that takes the PvE experience even further. This is for 12 players, split into three squads, fighting to defend Kalona from huge moments.

The Coalition is planning a seasonal road map, with more details coming soon. An Open Beta weekend will also start on 6th August, with early access to this for Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscribers.

via Xbox Wire



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/gears-of-war-e-day-direct-everything-we-now-know/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Clowntown is an upcoming party game where clowning around is encouraged
item.content:encoded:
Batterystaple Games, the development team behind 30XX, has revealed its next project and this time it is a clown show. Clowntown is a multiplayer co-op party game in which two to eight players can play through 100 minigames where teamwork is needed to progress. At the end of the journey is a showcase with the Ringmaster.

Clowntown does have an element of competition too with the Contest Mode, in which players are not working with each other but facing off against each other in the minigames. The minigames themselves evolve from simple to slightly stress inducing. One of those is the Spelling Bee series. It starts with spelling short words, but the second stage is called Spelling Wasp in which a 28 word needs to be spelt. The final stage is called Bees Spelling where you have to grab bees and use them to spell words.

Clowntown is going to be released on PC via Steam, but there is no release date yet.

Source: Press Release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/clowntown-is-an-upcoming-party-game-where-clowning-around-is-encouraged/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Toys For Bob is Back With Spyro: A Realm Beyond, Arriving on PC and Consoles in Spring 2027
item.content:encoded:
Today's Xbox Games Showcase 2026 was a continuation of Xbox boss Asha Sharma's efforts to achieve the 'return of Xbox' by bringing back Xbox console exclusives, though that wasn't the only notable return in the show. Activision revealed the return of the most popular purple dragon in gaming, as Spyro: A Realm Beyond brings the franchise back with its first new game in years, and yes, it's Toys For Bob behind it.

It'll arrive at some point in Spring 2027, and fans of Spyro on PlayStation and Nintendo devices don't have to worry about any exclusivity. It'll arrive on all current-gen PC and consoles (Switch 2 included), though if you're already playing on Xbox, you'll also be able to play it through Xbox Game Pass.

We last saw Spyro in a quality remake trilogy, the Reignited Trilogy, which first arrived back in 2018 and was also developed by Toys For Bob. A fourth Spyro game from Toys For Bob has been rumoured for some time, though with the studio's breakup with Activision putting a lot into question, it wasn't certain where exactly the team's relationship with Activision and Microsoft would go.

Even after we learned that the studio's first game as a recently independent team would be published by Microsoft and Activision, whether the studio would be able to successfully pitch a Spyro or Crash Bandicoot game was unclear, even if they were the clear choice for the team to carry both franchises forward.

In an Xbox Wire interview that went live alongside the reveal, Toys For Bob studio head Paul Yan admitted how fans of the franchise have been left in limbo for so long, and how much it means for the team to finally show off what they've been working on.

"I know the wait has been excruciating but we’re so thrilled to finally come out of stealth and confirm that a new Spyro game is indeed what we’ve been up to these past few years," Yan said.

"The little purple dragon means so much to us – we have a long history together; Spyro was the main inspiration that led to the innovations of the Skylanders series that we pioneered, and the remake of the original three games built from the ground up in the Spyro Reignited Trilogy – which reintroduced Spyro to a modern audience. Ever since then, we’ve been dreaming of what an original new adventure would look like, and that dream has manifested in Spyro: A Realm Beyond."

As you can see by the quick look at gameplay we get in the trailer, A Realm Beyond will be a big leap forward for Spyro, with improved flying instead of just gliding, deeper exploration, and even a seemingly more mature story for the first time in the franchise.

"A big reason why I love Spyro games is that they are ultimately joyful, dream-like experiences full of charming, quirky friends and Spyro: A Realm Beyond certainly builds on that important foundation. In the showcase announcement trailer, you may have noticed that we are introducing the threat of a new antagonist in this story, and his presence will raise the stakes. He will challenge Spyro’s abilities and push him to new emotional depths that will see Spyro grow as a hero. It’s a story full of heart and I can’t wait for you to experience this new journey," Yan said.

"Spyro is back. He is headlining a brand-new adventure. And I can’t wait for you to experience how he soars to new heights in Spyro: A Realm Beyond. This game was developed with love for Spyro and more importantly, for you."

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/spyro-a-realm-beyond-revealed-release-date-xbox-games-showcase-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Announced at PC Gaming Show 2026
item.content:encoded:
Hot on the heels of the Xbox Games Showcase, the annual PC Gaming Show started, promising to show us some of the most interesting titles coming to PC Gaming in the future as presented, as always by Day[9].

Note: This is a live updated recap of everything announced during the event. If an announcement isn't shown here yet, you may try refreshing and it will be there or will be soon. Additional information and trailers may be added at the end or as time permits. All of it is is in essentially chronological order.

The show opened up with a sitcom like opening sequence, and set in a living room you might see in a 90s sitcom, with Sean and Mica bantering a bit before going to 'host the PC Gaming Show'. Mica explains that they were channeling nostalgia, and two hours of trailers.

Star Trek Outposts Unknown Announced

The first world premiere was a look at a new owrld and people building things in it with what appears to be a Star Trek setting as it is revealed to be Star Trek: Outposts Unknown, a city builder type game set in the Trek universe with a demo available now on Steam.

Hack 95 Demo Available Now

Next is more 90s nostalgia with a game that looks like you're hacking 90s internet, and fighting the man with a user interface that seems right out of Windows 95 or 98... and with the name Hack 95 it would seem that it's meant to be Windows 95. It has a demo available on Steam now.

Relic Reveals Company of Heroes Definitive Edition

Next up is some cleaned up images and pretty pictures as Relic reveals Company of Heroes Definitive Edition, which will have new mod support, work on modern PCs, and lots of new bells and whistles.

Red Kiss Announced

Anime styled characters stand out in our next trailer as we see different characters in conflict in a potentially interesting and stylish game set in cold war Berlin. Red Kiss is taking inspiration from titles like Citizen Sleeper as your character uses psychic powers to send vampires on different missions and decide what it is you want to accomplish.

Arcane Kitchen Wants Good Eats 

Gordon Ramsey or a soundalike is on for a magical cooking game, where you can earn Mithral stars from Gorgon Ramsay and other celebrity chef knockoffs. It combines deck-building and cooking sim, and has a demo available now.

Serious Sam Shatterverse Brings more High Octane Energy

Sam is st ill not very serious in Serious Sam Shatterverse as we see shooting and running about in a roguelite fashion with co-op play.

Control Resonant Continues To Lurk Many Shows

Another new Control Resonant trailer showed up at the PC Gaming Show, showing us a new look at Remedy's game. This trailer told us about a new threat facing Manhatten: The Patterning a force that wants to change things into new patterns that want to make everything similar. 

Sated Revealed

Get ready for more cooking... but this isn't going to be getting any mithral stars as a roguelite with very very weird ingrediants you are cooking together and making oddities, and need gold to combine things.

Gone Feral is another innovative co-operative game

Everything is a weapon for you, and your friends in Gone Feral with different runs providing different experience. Gone Feral is described as a 'rogue-like assassination game'.

Abiotic Factor DLC Revealed

Plenty of people have enjoyed Abiotic Factor, so now those players can look forward to some more down at the beach with Entropic Break.

There are no Ghosts at the Grand Is an Oddity To see

Looking into the mystery of what happened to your dad, you are exploring a very weird town, where things are just going on odd. You're trying to avoid too much suspicion from the locals as you dig into the oddities that are clearly all over. Including you know the fact that the neighborhood cop is going to die in 30 days somehow.

Virtue and a Sledgehammer Demo Available Now

Continuing in the weird game area, Virtue and a Sledgehammer is another one of the like the has a demo available now, just before Steam Next Fest.

Happy Bastards is a Tactical, Tactless Experience

If the filters of the modern world bug you, Happy Bastards might be the game as the characters seem to have relatively little tact at most. Joining that is that its a tactical RPG type game where you make moves on a board and active skills

Cassette Beasts 2002 Announced

Cassette Beasts 2002 was the next world premiere and had very saturday morning carton vibe feel to it, along with a system derivative of Pokemon.

2 Fights 2 Tight Spaces Shadowdropped right now

From the Fights in Tight Spaces and Knights in Tight Spaces people comes a multiplayer examination of that concept with a new look and polish that we haven't previously seen. It is available right now.

Stronghold 4 Revealed

Ben Starr came to reveal the upcoming Stronghold 4 where you need to rebuild strongholds, and lead your people, while dealing with various events and enemy forces ... or be the enemy force yourself. Stronghold 4 releases this year and a demo releasing June 23rd.

Wardens of Avalon is a new Arthur Legend

An Arthurian game is up now, with who appears to be Morgana talking about the 'Twilight Knights', a dozen knights who shall fight to save the realm now that the knights of the round table have all been corrupted and failed.

Planet Zoo 2 First Gameplay

We see the first Planet Zoo 2 gameplay, with many of the features you would expect after Frontier that we've seen between Project Zoo and Jurassic Park Evolution. We will see new things like aviaries so you can raise birds. Planet Zoo 2 release date is set for October 13th.

Revamp Invites You To Be Evil

From developers on Moonlighter and Catacylismo comes another title seeking to cross genres in an idd way, as Revamp seems to be something closer to a Dungeon Master style experience.

Maximum Thunderness May be for you if you don't like your ear drums

If you like loud music or want to experience a game where your opponent won't knock you out because you're likely as annoyed as they are. It mixes this with loud, stylish visuals and all sort of other little interesting things.

Behaviour Interactive Reveals Mr Magpie's Harmless Games

Combine horror, rogue-lite and card games, Behaviour Interactive decided to take its shot at the Balatro pot with Mr Magpie's Harmless Games that is a take on Minesweeper.

Thief: The Dark Project Remastered announced

Nightdive Studios and Eidos Montrol announced Thief: The Dark Project Remastered, a remastering of the late-90s stealth immersive game starring Garrett. While not hugely commercially successful, Thief: The Dark Project is a cult classic, and is the type of project that Nightdive has become known for taking on - bringing old games that people missed out on for the first time. They have the VA for Garrett is back to reprise his role, and they are going to work on making all the technical details feel modern with options like 120 FPS support.

Duskers 2 Announced

Duskers 2 was announced, as the narrator has to stay behind on the ship, to give the various familiies their best chances of survival by spreading them out on a variety of planets. Reprising at least some of the distinctive graphics of Duskers, it appears to be quiet interesting.

Beast of Reincarnation is still in showing

Gamefreak is continuing to show the Beast of Reincarnation, their biggest budget non-Pokemon game possibly ever. It is an open-world and you can see the action adventure third person journeying, animal companions, and lots of foes to fight as well as areas to explore in the footage. It is set to release on August 4th, 2026.

Pipes.exe is another 90s set game

The trailer for System 16  showed us their system training manual in theory. Here we see that it invovles entering computer space, and battle bugs, and play with friends to uncover mysteries.

Dave the Diver in the Jungle Got A Partial Live Action Trailer

With Dave the Diver into the Jungle DLC releasing on June 18th, Mintrocket decided to showcase the game here and their trailer was a bit odd, partnering with The Asylum for a live-action segment of the trailer. There was some new gameplay showcased as well and some sights you can expect. Do note that Dave the Diver was updated to disclose it uses generative AI for localization, and this also applies to Into the Jungle.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/everything-announced-at-pc-gaming-show-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The 7 biggest storylines from Summer Game Fest 2026
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 edition of Summer Game Fest just wrapped up, and it was surprisingly hectic. The nearly week-long event came at a challenging time for the games industry, and for the most part the big keynotes were used as a chance to show some strength through major announcements, while largely ignoring pesky details like hardware price hikes and the mess of the live-service space.

Both Sony and Microsoft used their showcases as a way to confirm they’re refocusing on tried-and-true strategies like exclusive games and single-player blockbusters. Meanwhile, every publisher in existence seemed to be avoiding going up against Grand Theft Auto VI on the release calendar, and there were some very welcome game announcements, particularly if you’re a fan of Final Fantasy or Persona.

If you couldn’t keep up with everything live, here are the most important storylines to catch up on.

After an ill-fated — and very expensive — foray into live-service games, it appears that Sony’s gaming division has a renewed focus on the single-player epics it’s known for. The company’s showcase was dominated by Insomniac’s Wolverine and the surprise announcement of God of War Laufey.

The next Grand Theft Auto wasn’t featured in any of the SGF showcases, but its presence was still felt. While lots of games got release dates, virtually none of them were during November, which just so happens to be when GTA VI launches. Instead, we have a very busy September and plenty of titles pushed into 2027.

Alan Wake studio Remedy hit a snag with the disastrous launch of the multiplayer shooter FBC: Firebreak. But based on our time with the upcoming sequel Control Resonant, it appears the developer is getting back to what it’s best at: mind-bending single-player action games.

Indie duo Metanet is back with yet another return to its N series of platformers, but this time the focus is on multiplayer. And for fans of the hidden object game Hidden Folks, it’s also getting a sequel, which will launch a full decade after the original.

We knew it was coming, and now it’s official: The third and final installment of the FFVII remake trilogy is coming. It’s called Revelation, and it launches next spring across basically all platforms simultaneously. And yes, Queen’s Blood is coming back.

It’s been a long wait since Persona 5, and it’ll likely still be a while longer. Atlus confirmed Persona 6 exists, but the developer didn’t provide much in the way of detail, suggesting that the RPG is still fairly early in development.

After years of pushing on a multiplatform strategy, Microsoft is reversing course — at least a little bit. Its next big Xbox Game Studios title, Gears of War: E-Day, will be an Xbox console exclusive, whereas many expected it to come to the PS5, much like last year’s Gears remake. However, outside of Gears, many first-party titles from Xbox — like Fable and Halo — are still coming to PlayStation, so it’s unclear just how significant this change is.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Andrew Webster


https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/945445/summer-game-fest-2026-biggest-stories
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox’s Strategy On Exclusives Is More Confusing Than Ever
item.content:encoded:
Which Microsoft first-party games will be Xbox console exclusives? Which ones will eventually come to PlayStation 5? More than two years into this unprecedented experiment, it’s harder to know than ever.

The company revealed during its big summer showcase on June 7 that Gears of War: E-Day will not come to PS5 after all, despite a port of the game reportedly already existing on a drive somewhere at The Coalition’s offices. Clockwork Revolution, an upcoming RPG from inXile Entertainment which Microsoft acquired in 2018, will be an Xbox console exclusive as well.

But Fable is still coming to PS5. And Senua, the latest game in the Hellblade franchise from Ninja Theory which was just announced during today’s showcase, will be multiplatform as well. Even State of Decay 3 is coming to PS5. What exactly is going on here? How is Microsoft deciding what to make exclusive and what to put on Sony’s rival console platform?

“We want people to choose XBOX because of great games and experiences,” new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma wrote on X after the showcase wrapped up. “That also means giving you something that was made for XBOX. Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution will be XBOX console exclusives!”

Microsoft elaborated on this philosophy ever so slighting in a new Xbox Wire blog post by the blog’s editor, Joe Skrebels. “These are not timed exclusives,” he wrote, referring to both Gears and Clockwork. “Games already announced for multiplatform releases will stick to that plan – we’re committed to investing in and growing XBOX both on console and beyond.”

The obvious takeaway here is that Xbox is going back to the drawing board when it comes to exclusivity. That’s understandable. There are new people in charge and they will have different ideas about what to prioritize and how to grow and maintain a successful gaming platform in 2026 than their predecessors did.

But reading between the lines, it seems like Xbox is embarking on another experiment to see if a few exclusives here and there can retain the good will of its hardcore fanbase while games that those fans care less about, like Senua, or that have a better chance of resonating with a PlayStation fanbase, like Fable, stick with the multiplatform approach.

The big question now is what this means for the future of Halo. While Halo: Campaign Evolved was already announced for PS5, and is thus sticking to that promise, it’s unclear what this evolving position will mean for the previous claim that the Halo franchise overall will be on PlayStation “going forward.”



https://kotaku.com/xboxs-strategy-on-exclusives-is-more-confusing-than-ever-2000703739
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hand-drawn life simulation adventure game Vivarium launches in 2027 for Xbox Series, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Step into the role of 11-year-old Jenny.

Hand-drawn life simulation adventure game Vivarium, first announced in December 2023, will launch for Xbox Series and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and Microsoft Store in 2027, publisher Serenity Forge and developers Studio Meadowflower, Crunchyroll, and Shochiku Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Serenity Forge:

Step into a summer that never ends.

You wake up in the golden haze of late afternoon. The air is warm, cicadas hum somewhere in the distance, and sunlight spills across your bedroom.

And outside your window… there’s a tiny world waiting.

You play as 11-year-old Jenny, a bright and energetic resident of the terrarium.

But this isn’t just her story.

Somewhere, buried deep under the summer days and small-town routines, is another life.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Unravel the Mysteries of the Terrarium

Beneath the nostalgic atmosphere of this close-knit town lies a hidden truth. Explore a “perfect” pocket world to reveal its secrets and progress through a non-linear narrative shaped by your choices.

Meet Dear Friends You’ve Always Needed

Jenny’s charming neighborhood is inhabited by a charismatic cast of characters, each with their own branching and dynamic storylines:

  • A reclusive woman slowly becoming something else
  • A shopkeeper that gives daily album recommendations
  • A funny postal worker who drinks like a fish, and is a fish
  • A nosy retiree birdwatcher, who keeps tabs on the other townies
  • A wandering fortune teller who seems to know too much

Live Your Days as Dreams

Days in Vivarium are synced to the player’s real-world calendar, allowing for daily new discoveries while unraveling its mysterious narrative. Completing major story events will progress time, but every night provides endless slice-of-life comfort and time that can be spent:

  • Cooking new recipes and tending to your garden
  • Collecting stickers, vinyl records, and classic novels to enjoy
  • Crafting items and decorating your cozy home
  • Repairing equipment and preparing for tomorrow’s adventure

Sprout quickly, little tree…but can this small world contain you?

Watch the announcement trailer below. View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Publisher Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/hand-drawn-life-simulation-adventure-game-vivarium-launches-in-2027-for-xbox-series-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Metro 2039 Trailer Shows Off Impressive Visuals And Gameplay
item.content:encoded:
Metro 2039 is the fourth mainline game in the ongoing Metro series, and though we've been aware of its existence, today offered the first substantial public look at its gameplay. Along with showing off the visuals and environments, we also get to see gunplay and other mechanics, and learn about the Fuhrer Hunter, "the man who has washed the Metro in lies and propaganda to gain the ultimate power."

For more on Metro 2039, you can read our extensive preview on the game right here. Metro 2039 does not yet have an exact release date, but it is coming to Xbox Series X/S, PlayStation 5, and PC February of 2027.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/new-metro-2039-trailer-shows-off-impressive-visuals-and-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vivarium Is Looking Fresh In New Gameplay Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Amid a sea of zombies, shooters, and high-violence reveals at today’s Xbox Games Showcase, Vivarium was one of a small handful of games that offered a decidedly cozier aesthetic. Originally revealed back in 2023, this is the first time we've seen the game in action for some time.

Crafted by Studio Meadowflower and Serenity Forge, the game explores a small terrarium hidden away in the sun room of a small house somewhere in rural America. But within the glass house, a whole town of activity is unfolding. 

Players control 11-year-old girl, Jenny, as she navigates her days of endless summer in a world inspired by classic anime aesthetics like those seen in early Miyazaki films. Players talk to the electic (sometimes animal-like) townspeople, cook recipes, tend garden, collect stickers, decorate their home, and investigate a hidden secret to your tiny world. 

Vivarium looks beautiful and approachable. The game is targeting a 2027 release on Xbox Series X/S and PC. 



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/vivarium-is-looking-fresh-in-new-gameplay-reveal
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Doom: The Dark Ages Is Getting An Expansion Called Revelations In July
item.content:encoded:
Revealed during the 2026 Xbox Game Showcase, Revelations is a new expansion for Doom: The Dark Ages that will be available on July 7. Id is using terms like "Hell freezes over" to describe the expansion and writes, "Wounded and betrayed, the Slayer is thrust into a merciless purgatory only escapable by confronting haunting truths and forging new strength with the help of a mysterious ally. He must ascend from the prison of his mind and fight an abomination of the gods to set his followers on the path to freedom."

The trailer for the expansion, expectedly, shows off violent and brutal first-person combat with a voiceover that says, "This place is your pain given shape." The Slayer is also using a new weapon, a glowing javelin, that appears to replace his shield.

Again, Doom: The Dark Ages' Revelations expansion will be available on July 7. You can read Game Informer's Doom: The Dark Ages review by following the link.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/doom-the-dark-ages-is-getting-an-expansion-called-revelations-in
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox's Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution are not timed exclusives, as PlayStation 5 misses out on upcoming releases
item.content:encoded:
We just got another look at Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution at this evening's Xbox Games Showcase. While many had assumed that Gears of War: E-Day specifically would be coming to PlayStation 5 as well as part of Xbox's recent drive to release its game across multiple platforms, that is not the case.

But, it is not just that these games aren't coming to PlayStation consoles yet. It looks like they aren't coming to PS5 at all.

In a blog post following this evening's showcase, Xbox Wire's Editor-in-Chief Joe Skrebels confirmed both Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution aren't timed exclusives. They are just exclusives. This is part of the company's "focus on the return of Xbox," the post reads.

Skrebels added that games which have already been announced for multiplatform releases "will stick to that plan", and the company is "committed to investing in and growing Xbox both on console and beyond".

Until very recently, Xbox had been adding more of its games to additional platforms, such as PlayStation and Nintendo's Switch family of consoles. This included the likes of Grounded, Sea of Thieves and Hi-Fi Rush. Microsoft then went on to release the likes of Forza Horizon 5 and Indiana Jones and the Great Circle on PlayStation.

However, newly-appointed Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has been steadily leaving her mark on the brand, and has evidently decided that those on Microsoft's consoles need some proper exclusives once again. And so Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution are not coming to PlayStation 5.

Other Xbox Studio's games are still going to be available on multiple platforms, though, such as Playground's Fable. For everything else from this evening, be sure to check out Lottie's roundup: Everything announced in Microsoft's Xbox Games Showcase.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/xboxs-gears-of-war-e-day-and-clockwork-revolution-are-not-timed-exclusives-as-playstation-5-misses-out-on-upcoming-releases
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Be a cheeky bird thief in Bad Magpie
item.content:encoded:
Untitled Goose Game fans better prepare themselves for more avian shenanigans with the reveal of Bad Magpie, coming to Xbox Series X|S and PC in 2027. Game Pass Ultimate on day one, too.

Coming from UK-based studio Milktooth, there’s a little bit of heartbreak to the story of Bad Magpie. Magpie has been abandoned by her flock as she lives with just a single wing. So, she starts to compulsively try to collect trinkets in the hope of winning the affection of a fallen star.

It’s a non-linear open world that you can run around in, pecking, stealing, meddling with things that you find.

Milktooth is an independent games studio founded by artist Catherine Unger (Tangle Tower, Gibbon: Beyond the Trees), designer Daisy Fernandez (Card Shark, As Dusk Falls), and developer George Batchelor (Bird Alone, South of the Circle).



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/be-a-cheeky-bird-thief-in-bad-magpie/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ninja Theory’s Next Game Is Senua, a Full Action-Adventure Built Around What Fans Actually Wanted After Hellblade II: More Gameplay
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps the biggest surprise of the Xbox Games Showcase 2026 was the unveiling of Senua, an action/adventure game centered on the protagonist of the Hellblade series. In an Xbox Wire blog post, Ninja Theory says the game follows on from Senua’s Saga: Hellblade II, but it is intentionally a different kind of experience and should not be treated as Hellblade III.

Unlike the previous two Hellblade titles, this is an out-and-out action/adventure. The studio's goal is to keep the intimate storytelling, high production values, and emotional tone that defined Hellblade, while expanding the scope into a broader action-adventure framework. That means deeper gameplay, greater player agency, and more traditional elements such as combat, traversal, puzzle-solving, and exploration. The objective is to satisfy both Hellblade fans and players who want a fuller action-adventure game.

Studio Head Dom Matthews claimed that the title Senua reflects that this is “fresh and new and different,” with a different intention from the previous two games. He describes the project as additive: taking what worked in Hellblade and adding the kinds of systems players expect from a premium action-adventure title. The article also makes clear that Ninja Theory spent time reading user reviews and forums, and the team concluded that many players loved the presentation, tone, and atmosphere of Hellblade II but wanted more gameplay and more agency.

A major behind-the-scenes change is that Ninja Theory brought the entire studio together on one project for the first time in over 12 years, since the controversial DmC: Devil May Cry. Matthews says the studio had around 85 creatives on this game, which is still relatively small by AAA standards. This was also possible because of the cancellation of Project Mara, which allowed the studio to concentrate its talent on Senua and realize its full potential.

Senua's structure is built around a fairly even split between combat, traversal, and puzzle solving. Matthews explicitly says players should not expect a combat-heavy game that is “two-thirds combat,” but rather a more balanced action-adventure experience. The key design goal is to blend these elements so the game feels broad without losing the focus and quality associated with Hellblade. That shift is also reflected in the game’s more open layout and increased player freedom.

Enhanced Combat

Combat is one of the biggest expansions. Senua will be fighting multiple enemies at once, a major change from the more intimate, usually one-versus-one encounters in previous games. Ninja Theory says the combat is built around tactical choice, meaning players can choose how to engage, whether to sneak in stealthily or to attack directly. The trailer shows Senua using a much broader set of tools, including a long axe, short axe, dual-wielding, and throwing weapons. Weapons are not just cosmetic; they are meant to be picked up and used dynamically.

Matthews explains that some weapons can be carried with you while others are temporary tools you use in the moment. He gives an example of a player sneaking around a fight, grabbing a weapon from the environment, using it to take out a high-threat enemy, and then returning to the rest of the battle. Senua can also dual-wield with almost all of these weapons, allowing players to express themselves more freely in combat.

The game also introduces Focus Abilities, which are tied to Senua's beliefs and understandings over the course of the journey. One example is her ability to shatter reality, which can open new spaces in the world and also serve as a crowd-control tool in combat. These abilities are used across traversal, puzzle solving, and fighting, so they are not isolated mechanics but a core part of how the player interacts with the world.

Greater Exploration

The game's map is about twice the size of Hellblade II’s, yet it's still a single map. Matthews emphasizes that this is not an open world game, but a linear story told through interconnected locations. Players will revisit familiar places during the story, but the world is more open than before and designed to reward time spent exploring. This is a key distinction: the game is bigger and freer, but still intentionally guided rather than fully sandbox-like. The studio also added that there will be fantastical boss fights.

Exploration is meant to be meaningful, not just scenic. Players will discover secrets if they are perceptive, and some of those secrets are tied to Senua developing new beliefs that alter how she perceives the world. One example is a tree that initially looks insignificant but later gains special meaning and power, allowing access to a new area.

Traversal is also significantly expanded. Ninja Theory says movement will feel freer and snappier, with increased speed and more player choice over how to navigate spaces. Players can now vault, climb, and jump more often, and the world includes much more verticality and multi-level spaces than in the Hellblade games. Senua’s ability to focus and perceive reality differently still matters, but now it is combined with a more versatile movement kit.

The story takes place after both Hellblade I and Hellblade II and is set in Purgatory, specifically Senua’s vision of her childhood homeland. She is trapped between life and death and is seeking the afterlife in order to reunite with loved ones she has lost. Her belief is that healing the wounds of her life will bring peace and unlock the gate to the afterlife. Returning players will recognize characters, themes, and story elements, but as you'd expect, Senua is also designed to welcome new players without requiring prior knowledge.

One of the game's most surprising aspects is the relatively fast turnaround: Hellblade II was released in 2024, and Senua is scheduled for 2027. Matthews says the pace is deliberate, not rushed, because the studio had the tech, the pipeline, the team, and a clear concept, allowing them to move quickly. Development on the new game had already begun by September 2024, shortly after Hellblade II shipped in May.

Senua will be available on Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, cloud, Steam, and PlayStation 5. It will also be included with Game Pass and support Xbox Play Anywhere.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/ninja-theory-senua-action-adventure-hellblade-ii/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
